meta.scoring_note: Elitez (Discounter + FlashCart) scored as combined line. Discounter is dorm-delivery near-expiry FMCG; FlashCart is in-person flash-sale wellness. Pre-launch / early-stage scoring against MVP scope.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez breaks the cluster on dorm_delivery_convenience, surplus_clearance_depth, and bulk_basket_economics — three axes the SG mass-grocery cluster cannot follow without rebuilding their dorm logistics and accepting near-expiry inventory risk; we deliberately concede trust_brand_familiarity and returns_handling, where 30-year incumbents (FairPrice, Mustafa) own buyer mindshare.

rubrics.price_floor: 0=premium-only · 1=at retail · 2=10-20% off retail · 3=20-40% off retail · 4=40-60% off retail · 5=60-80%+ off retail (clearance / near-expiry)
rubrics.dorm_delivery_convenience: 0=no delivery to dorms · 1=address-only delivery (still walk to gate) · 2=island-wide delivery, no dorm-specific routing · 3=delivers to a few dorms ad-hoc · 4=scheduled dorm-route service · 5=dedicated dorm-block-level drop, batched cutoff, multi-language coordination
rubrics.surplus_clearance_depth: 0=no clearance · 1=occasional yellow-sticker shelf · 2=in-store clearance corner · 3=monthly online clearance event · 4=permanent surplus SKU programme · 5=core inventory model is near-expiry/clearance
rubrics.wellness_vitamin_range: 0=none · 1=a few SKUs · 2=basic OTC + a few vitamins · 3=full vitamin aisle · 4=branded wellness depth (Centrum/Caltrate/Blackmores) · 5=full pharmacist-grade wellness assortment
rubrics.bulk_basket_economics: 0=single-item ecom only · 1=basket but no break · 2=free delivery threshold S$60+ · 3=free delivery threshold S$40-60 · 4=bulk multi-pack pricing + threshold · 5=basket-economics is the core pitch (S$40 free delivery + bulk packs + group-buy)
rubrics.paynow_cashless: 0=cash only · 1=card only · 2=card+e-wallet · 3=PayNow + cards · 4=PayNow + cards + WhatsApp checkout · 5=PayNow native + multi-rail (PayNow/cards/wallets/cash on delivery)
rubrics.trust_brand_familiarity: 0=unknown · 1=under 1yr brand · 2=local startup brand <3yr · 3=known regional brand · 4=10+yr SG brand · 5=30+yr SG household name with worker recognition
rubrics.returns_refund_handling: 0=no returns · 1=refunds case-by-case · 2=published returns policy · 3=published policy + clear damage replacement · 4=app/portal returns flow · 5=in-store + online seamless returns with same-day refund

segments.name: Bangladeshi construction worker
segments.descriptor: Dorm-resident, ~S$700/mo, sends most home; Sunday rest day

segments.name: Filipino service / domestic worker
segments.descriptor: Dorm or live-in; English-comfortable; Christian wellness culture

segments.name: SG HDB-heartland value shopper
segments.descriptor: Local SG household, pension/working-class, Sheng Siong shopper

segments.name: Employer welfare buyer (HR)
segments.descriptor: HR / admin in construction/F&B/cleaning, procuring bulk staff welfare

segments.name: Dormitory canteen operator
segments.descriptor: B2B SKU bulk-buyer for dorm canteens; weekly replenishment

segments.name: Community / NGO buyer
segments.descriptor: MWC, HOME, charity packs for migrant workers

segments.name: Off-Sunday weekday rest-day shopper
segments.descriptor: Service workers on Tue/Wed rest day; dorm shops shut by 9pm

needs.name: Lowest price per kg / per unit
Dormitory delivery (no walk to bus stop)

attack_plans.niche_name: Dorm-block delivery wedge for Bangladeshi construction workers (Sunday-cutoff bulk staples)
attack_plans.icp: Bangladeshi construction-worker permit-holder living in a PBM dorm in Tuas/Soon Lee/Toh Guan, S$700/mo income, sends 60% home, currently spends Sunday on a bus to Mustafa for rice/oil/dal.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: MOM 2025: ~340k Work Permit holders in PBM/CTQ dorms; Bangladeshi share ~120k. Assume S$60/mo addressable wallet on rice/oil/lentils/instant noodles × 120k workers × 12 months = S$86.4M. Apply 60% reachable share (Tuas/Sungei Kadut/Soon Lee/Toh Guan dorm clusters we can route weekly) = S$51M annual TAM.
attack_plans.why_gap: FairPrice/Sheng Siong/RedMart all delivery-route to addresses, not dorm blocks — security and aggregation friction at PBM gates makes single-flat drops uneconomical. Mustafa pulls workers TO the store. ChampionMart serves a few dorms but at retail prices and limited SKUs. No incumbent has scored ≥3 on dorm_delivery × Bangla-language ordering — a structural gap because dorm-block routing requires a Sunday-batched logistics model and Bangla-speaking ops.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Three structural edges scoring 5 each: (1) dorm_delivery_convenience — Sunday-batched dorm-block routing built for the model, not retrofitted; (2) bulk_basket_economics — S$40+ free-delivery threshold engineered for the worker's wallet, group-buy among bunkmates; (3) surplus_clearance_depth — near-expiry inventory model means -50% on the staples that matter (rice 5kg/oil 5L/dal 2kg). Bangla-language WhatsApp ordering is the moat against any incumbent retro-fit.

gtm.channel: Dorm-operator partnerships (Centurion, Mini Environment, Westlite) + Bangla-language WhatsApp groups seeded via dorm-block leaders + posters in Bangla/Tamil at PBM common areas + MWC partnership for trust-signal.
gtm.pitch: Sunday cutoff. Dorm-block delivery. Bangla-language ordering. Half the price of Mustafa, no bus fare.
gtm.pricing: S$40 minimum order = free delivery to your dorm block; S$25-39 = S$3 delivery; sub-S$25 not eligible. PayNow only. Bulk packs (5kg rice, 5L oil) priced 50-70% below retail via near-expiry sourcing.
gtm.content: Bangla-language 60-second WhatsApp video showing the dorm-block drop flow; weekly 'Sunday Cutoff' WhatsApp price-list (image, not text — works on low-end phones); dorm-block leader testimonial series; MWC co-branded explainer on consumer rights for migrant workers.

attack_plans.niche_name: Branded vitamin flash-sale events at dormitories (FlashCart × Centrum trust + 50% wedge)
attack_plans.icp: Filipino service / domestic worker (or Bangladeshi worker on Sunday rest day) who currently walks to Watsons/Guardian for Centrum/Caltrate at full retail; family-back-home gifts a major trigger for vitamin purchases.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~340k Work Permit + ~70k FDH = ~410k addressable migrant workforce in SG. Assume 30% buy branded vitamins at least once per year for self/family-back-home = 123k buyers. Average annual vitamin spend ~S$120 = S$14.7M. Round to S$14.4M as TAM ceiling for the FlashCart in-person flash-sale wedge.
attack_plans.why_gap: Watsons/Guardian own brand_familiarity (5/5) but never run >20% off Centrum/Caltrate — the branded vitamin SKU has high MSRP integrity in the SG mass-pharmacy channel. iHerb has price but requires English-comfortable digital buyer + cross-border patience. No competitor scores ≥3 on (worker segment × branded_wellness × event-based -50% pricing) because it requires direct manufacturer/parallel-import relationships that mass pharmacies cannot disrupt without breaking their MSRP discipline. Structural gap: an event-based pop-up doesn't cannibalise Watsons' aisle, and parallel-import sourcing keeps margin viable at -50%.
attack_plans.why_we_win: FlashCart leans on three Elitez Group asymmetries: (1) Elitez has dorm-operator relationships (sister-company HR/staffing access) — set up flash-sale tables INSIDE dorm common rooms on rest-day evenings, no rent; (2) parallel-import + near-expiry sourcing on Centrum/Caltrate enables 50% off retail without margin collapse; (3) PayNow-at-table point-of-sale cuts cash-handling friction. Combined our_score 4 on branded_wellness × dorm_delivery (event-as-delivery) — vs. competitor max 3 on this exact pair.

gtm.channel: Direct dorm-operator MOUs (Centurion + Westlite first), monthly flash-sale calendar pinned at dorm canteens, WhatsApp 'tomorrow at 7pm Tuas Block C' broadcast lists by dorm, Sunday Lucky Plaza/Peninsula Plaza pop-ups for FDH segment.
gtm.pitch: Centrum 100s. S$45 at Watsons. S$22.50 today only at your dorm canteen. PayNow at the table, walk away with the bottle.
gtm.pricing: Flat 50% off retail on 8-12 SKU rotation (Centrum, Caltrate, Blackmores fish oil, Berocca, Vit C); PayNow at point of sale; no online catalogue (scarcity is the wedge).
gtm.content: Dorm-block flyer (Bangla/Tagalog/Tamil/English quad-lingual, A5 print); 30-sec WhatsApp video showing a worker holding the same bottle priced S$45 at Watsons vs S$22.50 at FlashCart; 'send health home' framing — angle the buy as remittance-of-vitamins to family back home.

attack_plans.niche_name: Employer welfare-procurement combo (HR bulk staff-care basket, single PO+invoice, dorm-block drop)
attack_plans.icp: HR/admin manager at a SG construction firm / cleaning company / F&B chain employing 50-500 work-permit staff; currently does ad-hoc Mustafa runs or NTUC Business orders for staff Hari Raya / Deepavali / CNY gifting and routine welfare baskets.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: MOM data: ~5,500 SG companies employ 20+ work-permit holders. Assume 40% (2,200 firms) do at least quarterly welfare-pack procurement at S$30/worker × 80 workers avg × 4x/year = S$9,600/firm/year. 2,200 × S$9,600 = S$21.1M ceiling. Apply 45% reachable share given competition with FairPrice Business = S$9.6M annual TAM.
attack_plans.why_gap: FairPrice Business handles the PO/invoice workflow well (3-4 score) but does not deliver to dorm blocks — HR has to truck pallets from FairPrice DC to the dorm themselves, OR the workers carry packs back from the office. No competitor scores ≥3 on the combined cell of (hr_welfare_buyer × dorm_delivery × employer_po_invoice). Structural gap because the workflow needs both B2B billing rails AND dorm-block last-mile — incumbents have one or the other, never both.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Three Elitez asymmetric advantages: (1) Elitez Group's existing HR/staffing book of business (1,000+ HR contacts already from the staffing arm) means warm cross-sell at zero CAC; (2) Discounter's dorm-block drop already runs on Sundays — adding a Friday HR-batch slot is a marginal logistics cost; (3) Single PayNow Corporate / GIRO + GST-compliant invoice workflow purpose-built for HR finance teams. Our_score 4 on (hr_welfare_buyer × dorm_delivery), 5 on (× bulk_basket_econ), 4 on (× employer_po_invoice) — the only competitor profile spanning all three.

gtm.channel: Direct cross-sell to Elitez Group's existing 1,000+ HR contacts + cold outbound to top-200 work-permit-employer firms via MOM data + LinkedIn ABM to HR Directors at construction/cleaning/F&B firms 50-500 headcount.
gtm.pitch: One PO. One GST invoice. We drop the welfare packs at your workers' dorm block on Friday — they don't carry them home, you don't run a bus. Half the price of FairPrice Business.
gtm.pricing: Welfare-Pack tiers: Bronze S$15/worker (rice 2kg + oil 1L + instant noodles 5pk + biscuit), Silver S$25/worker (+ vitamin pack), Gold S$40/worker (+ branded wellness flash-pack). 30-day GIRO terms for verified UEN. Dorm-block delivery free above 50-pack order.
gtm.content: Single-page B2B PDF brochure for HR (UEN + GST + sample invoice template); 1-pager case study with 1-2 launch firms (Centurion, BS Industrial); Hari Raya / Deepavali / CNY seasonal flash-procurement calendar emailed to HR list 8 weeks ahead of each festival; LinkedIn post series 'why your staff-welfare pallet costs 40% more than it should'.

_handoff_notes.to_agent_6_visualisation: Render 8-axis radar with Elitez line breaking the cluster on price_floor (5), dorm_delivery_convenience (5), surplus_clearance_depth (5), bulk_basket_economics (5), conceding deliberately on trust_brand_familiarity (2) and returns_refund_handling (1). Heatmap 8x8: green attack-eligible cells cluster heavily in (bangla_construction, filipino_service, hr_welfare_buyer, dorm_canteen_op, ngo_buyer, weekday_offrest) x (bulk_basket_econ, dorm_delivery, multilang_ux, whatsapp_mobile). Avoid rendering verdict strings in JSON — derive at render time from competitors[] count where score>=3.
_handoff_notes.to_agent_8_report: Cell Detail Appendix should inline EVERY green-cell (count 0-1 with our_score>=4) — there are 30+ such cells, all clustered around dorm-delivery and bulk-basket needs. Red cells (count 4+) are NONE in this matrix because the segments are under-served by mass grocery — instead, flag the AMBER cells where Elitez should NOT compete: (hdb_value_local x lowest_unit_price), (hdb_value_local x branded_wellness), (spass_off_dorm x branded_wellness), (filipino_service x branded_wellness), (spass_off_dorm x returns_damage).
_handoff_notes.deliberately_skip: Elitez should NOT pursue (hdb_value_local x branded_wellness) — Watsons/Guardian/NTUC own this with 30+ years of trust and HDB-heartland presence; our brand_familiarity score of 2 cannot close the gap inside 3 years. Similarly (spass_off_dorm x returns_damage) — RedMart's in-app refund flow sets a digital-native expectation we cannot match at a clearance-margin business model; concede this cell and lean into price + bulk.
